name: Agentic RAG — Autonomous Multi-Step Retrieval
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Agent reasoning engine — GPT-4o plans retrieval steps, synthesizes multi-hop answers, self-evaluates

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput, guaranteed latency for multi-step

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Hybrid search index — iterative retrieval with query reformulation, semantic reranking

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Agent orchestrator — manages reasoning loop, tool execution, state transitions

prod.note: 2 vCPU, 4GB, min 2 replicas for parallel agents

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Agent state persistence — conversation memory, retrieval history, reasoning traces

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys for OpenAI, AI Search admin keys, agent signing secrets

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Document corpus — source documents, chunked artifacts, agent tool outputs

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Agent tracing — step-by-step reasoning visibility, retrieval quality, token spend per query

services.name: Content Safety
services.purpose: Multi-step output moderation — validates each retrieval-synthesis cycle, not just final answer

optimization_tips: Cap agent reasoning steps at 5 iterations max — prevents runaway token consumption on ambiguous queries
Use GPT-4o-mini for query reformulation steps, GPT-4o only for final synthesis — saves 70% on intermediate steps
Cache intermediate retrieval results — if agent re-queries similar context, reuse previous search results
Implement early termination — if retrieval confidence > 0.9 after step 2, skip remaining iterations
Use AI Search semantic ranker only on final retrieval step — keyword search sufficient for exploratory steps
Set per-query token budgets (e.g., 10K tokens max per agent session) to prevent cost spikes on complex queries
